rationale_top3: Entertainment (16/20): Families praised the jumping pillow, two pools including adults-only and baby pool, Easter Bunny visits, outdoor movies with popcorn, and ice cream van. However, some noted games room equipment wasn't working and limited entertainment compared to expectations for the price point. Nature (18/20): Located directly across the road from Kinka Beach with easy beach access, surrounded by mature trees providing shade and interesting vegetation. Wildlife including peacocks and chickens roam the park. Beach stretches over 3km for dog walking, close to Emu Park and Great Keppel Island gateway. Cleanliness (14/15): Overwhelming praise for spotless amenities and crystal clean pools described as almost up to standard for heart surgery. Recent reviews note some decline with dirty cabin floors and laundry facilities needing attention, plus tiles requiring regrouting and bird bathroom issues.
rationale_honourable: Value (12/15) received mixed feedback with many calling it affordable at $48-50/night and value for money, though others felt $70+ per night disappointing. Site Size (8/10) praised for large slab sites not crammed in, but multiple complaints about specific small sites where caravans barely fit. Strong sentiment (9/10) with repeat visitors extending stays and returning for years, despite some negative reviews about staff rudeness.
water_fun: Two pools including adults-only, baby pool available
kids_play: Jumping pillow, Easter events, outdoor movies, scooter-friendly
pet_detail: Pet friendly with dogs allowed; beach has off-leash hours before 8am and after 4pm; dog washing in laundry machines is an ongoing issue
best_for: Families with young children and pets seeking beach access and clean facilities
executive_summary:
PARK: Ingenia Holidays Kinka Beach Yeppoon
SCORE: 78/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-20
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 106

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (16/20) — Families praised the jumping pillow, two pools (including adults-only and baby pool), Easter Bunny visits, outdoor movies with popcorn, and ice cream van. Some noted games room equipment wasn't working and limited entertainment compared to expectations, with one review disappointed by "blow up jumping pillow not much for the kids to do" for the price.

Nature (18/20) — Located directly across the road from Kinka Beach with easy beach access, surrounded by mature trees providing shade, "loads of interesting vegetation," and wildlife including peacocks and chickens roaming the park. Beach has "excessive low tide mark and mud flats" but stretches over 3km for dog walking. Close to Emu Park and Great Keppel Island gateway.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Overwhelming praise for "spotless amenities," "crystal clean pools," and cleanliness "almost up to standard for heart surgery." Recent reviews note decline with "floors in our room were filthy," laundry dryers full of dog fur, and "toilet/shower block had been cleaned all week" during one October 2024 stay. Tiles need regrouting with birds causing bathroom issues.

Value (12/15) — Mixed feedback with many calling it "value for money" and affordable at $48-50/night for families, but others felt $70+ per night "very disappointed for the price." Special deals noted (2 nights get 1 free). Gas refills and washing machines described as "pricey." Some booking flexibility issues with minimum night requirements.

Site Size (8/10) — "Large slab caravan sites" and "huge sites, not crammed in" praised by many, but multiple complaints about specific small sites (21, 86a) where "site was so small our car had to be park partially on the road" and "extremely narrow, only about 500mm wider than our van." Site allocation inconsistency noted.

Sentiment (9/10) — Strong repeat visitation with reviewers extending stays ("booked 6 nights, ended up staying 30"), returning for 3+ years, and "will definitely be back." However, several strongly negative 1-star reviews about "VERY RUDE" staff and management inflexibility hurt sentiment score. Transition to Ingenia management in July 2025 noted with concern.

Location (9/10) — "Perfect location" across from beach, 10 minutes to both Emu Park and Yeppoon, close to Great Keppel Island ferry. Some felt "out of the way" and "a bit far from shops." Walking to Emu Park requires walking along 80kph road with no footpath. Quiet location away from "hustle and bustle" appreciated.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Site size inconsistency is a significant issue with some families allocated extremely small sites (86a, 21) while larger sites sat vacant, creating frustration about "crammed in like sardines" and poor site allocation practices.
2. Recent maintenance decline noted after Ingenia takeover including dirty cabin floors, laundry facilities with broken dryers, shower tiles needing regrouting, unlocked amenities with bird droppings, and dog fur management issues.
3. Limited structured kids entertainment beyond jumping pillow and occasional events, with games room equipment not working, no camp kitchen in some areas, and disappointing facilities compared to price point for some families expecting more from Ingenia brand.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Easter Bunny going around the park the kids loved it", "Amenities always so spotless it seems a pity to use them", "jumping pillow, pool and not far to the beach", "outdoor movies with popcorn were great", "directly across the road from the beach", "site was so small our car had to be park partially on the road", "very disappointed for the price give it a miss", "value for money highly recommended"

rationale_top3: Exceptional water entertainment facilities with highly praised pools, waterslides, splash park, and jumping pillow that families consistently rate as among the best they've experienced, plus well-organized school holiday activities Convenient location alongside Causeway Lake with boat ramp access, beach across the road, and proximity to Byfield National Parks and Great Keppel Island ferry for families seeking both onsite fun and regional exploration Mixed but generally positive experiences with spacious sites available, modern ensuite options, and dedicated staff, though tempered by cleanliness inconsistencies and value concerns
rationale_honourable: Discovery Parks Coolwaters stands out for its exceptional water facilities—families consistently praise the pools, waterslides, splash park, and jumping pillow as "quite possibly one of the best we have seen." School holiday activities, movie nights, and organized entertainment keep kids engaged. The park offers spacious sites, modern ensuites, and an onsite cafe serving praised pizza. While some cabins show age and cleanliness inconsistency, the overall family-friendly vibe, lake access, and beach proximity make this a solid choice for families seeking active holiday entertainment.
water_fun: Outstanding pools, waterslides, splash park facilities
kids_play: Jumping pillow, playground, organized school holiday activities
pet_detail: Dogs only—cats and other pets not permitted despite general pet-friendly advertising; strict enforcement reported in reviews.
best_for: Active families with primary school-aged children who prioritize water play and onsite entertainment over pristine accommodation and want a base near beaches and nature parks.
executive_summary:
PARK: Discovery Parks - Coolwaters, Yeppoon
SCORE: 72/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-20
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 155

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (18/20) — Reviews consistently highlight exceptional water facilities with "best swimming pool water slide & water park for the little & big kid's," splash park, jumping pillow, tennis court, basketball court, movie nights, bingo, arts and crafts. School holiday activities described as "well thought through." Many families visited specifically for the waterpark and didn't leave the site.

Nature (14/20) — Park sits alongside Causeway Lake with boat ramp access, beach across the road, and proximity to Byfield National Parks, Great Keppel Island, and fishing spots. Some reviewers noted natural surroundings as "beautiful" though beach access from park not straightforward. Negative mentions of sewage/kelp smell from adjacent Causeway in some reviews.

Cleanliness (10/15) — Mixed feedback with recent reviews noting "amenities could have been cleaned more regularly," toilets sometimes "disgusting and unclean," mouse droppings, and ensuites needing "a really good clean." However, many also praised "always clean" amenities and "immaculately clean" park presentation, suggesting inconsistency.

Value (9/15) — Significant concerns about pricing: "extremely over priced for what you receive," "biggest rip off" for unpowered sites at $65/night, unexpected weekend price jumps ($30 to $47), and size-based pricing criticized. Some found deals on G'day Parks, but overall value perception negative relative to condition.

Site Size (7/10) — Reviews mention "nice big sites," "massive for our 24ft van," and "spacious" powered sites with ensuites. However, multiple complaints of being "packed in like sardines," sites on "unlevel dirt/mud," "dust bowl with no slab and no grass," and inconsistent site quality (grass vs sand vs dirt).

Sentiment (8/10) — Strong repeat visitation among satisfied families ("4th Christmas break," "third year visiting") with many "will definitely be back" comments. However, tempered by serious negative experiences including evictions, safety concerns, management conflicts, and "would not recommend" reviews from disappointed families.

Location (6/10) — Located at Kinka Beach, "10-15 mins into Yeppoon" and "nowhere close to Yeppoon" per reviews. Praised as convenient base for regional attractions (Great Keppel ferry, Byfield, fishing) but criticized for limited walkable amenities, difficult beach access from park, and isolation from town.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Significant cleanliness inconsistency—while some found amenities "always clean," multiple recent reviews reported unclean toilets, dirty ensuites needing deep cleaning, and maintenance issues suggesting quality control problems.
2. Poor value perception with unexpected pricing (weekend surcharges, size-based fees), high costs for basic unpowered sites on dirt/mud, and cabin condition not matching premium pricing or website photos.
3. Management and service issues including aggressive evictions (child on e-scooter, cat owners), inflexible refund policies even for medical emergencies, site allocation problems (near dump point, under trees causing van damage), and dismissive staff responses to legitimate complaints.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"quite possibly one of the best we have seen," "best swimming pool water slide & water park," "staff are AMAZING SUCH A FANTASTIC TEAM," "amenities could have been cleaned more regularly," "extremely over priced for what you receive," "NOT CHILD FRIENDLY," "packed in like sardines," "perfect family getaway"

rationale_top3: Strong family entertainment with jumping pillow, two swimming pools, playground, outdoor movies, games room with retro arcade, and pedal cart hire keeping kids engaged throughout their stay. Spacious, well-grassed sites particularly in back areas, with modern ensuite options providing comfortable accommodation for families seeking room to spread out. Pleasant coastal setting with palm trees, beach access within walking distance, and proximity to Great Keppel Island ferry terminal for island adventures.
rationale_honourable: NRMA Capricorn Yeppoon Holiday Park delivers a solid family camping experience with popular amenities like jumping pillows, two pools, and outdoor movies that keep children entertained. While cleanliness standards have declined in recent times according to multiple 2026 reviews, and the advertised water slide has been removed, the park still offers spacious sites, modern ensuite options, and a peaceful coastal setting near Great Keppel Island access. Families appreciate the friendly staff and well-maintained grounds, though the 11km distance from Yeppoon town and inconsistent maintenance mean it's better suited to self-sufficient families seeking a relaxed base rather than those wanting premium facilities or walkable conveniences.
water_fun: Two pools available, water slide removed
kids_play: Jumping pillow, playground, outdoor movie nights, games room with retro arcade, pedal cart hire
best_for: Families who prioritize site space and kids' entertainment amenities over proximity to town and who don't mind the 11km drive to Yeppoon for shopping and dining.
executive_summary:
PARK: NRMA Capricorn Yeppoon Holiday Park
SCORE: 72/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-23
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 162

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (16/20) — Strong family entertainment with jumping pillow consistently praised, two swimming pools, playground, outdoor movie nights, games room with retro arcade, pedal cart hire, and occasional food trucks and live music. However, the advertised water slide has been removed and recent reviews note declining activities compared to previous years with less communication about events.

Nature (14/20) — Pleasant natural setting with palm trees throughout, beach access 900m away (though beach not ideal for swimming), walking distance to Mulambin Beach, near Great Keppel Island ferry access, and turtle lookout nearby. Some sites have nice garden separation but overall natural environment is suburban coastal rather than pristine bushland.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Significant decline noted in recent 2026 reviews with multiple complaints about dirty amenities "haven't see cleaning for weeks", calcified showers, overgrown gardens, broken toilet buttons, and poor maintenance. Earlier reviews praised cleanliness as "spotless" and "cleaniest we've ever seen" but standards have clearly slipped.

Value (11/15) — Mixed feedback with some calling it "overpriced for what you get" and "too expensive at this park", while others found rates "amazingly cheap" and "excellent price". Ensuite sites seen as worth extra money, but removal of advertised features and declining standards have impacted value perception.

Site Size (8/10) — Consistently praised for "spacious sites", "good size sites", and "large sites". Ensuite sites described as large, flat and well-grassed. Some sites described as "snug" or tight, particularly closer to park center, but back sites very spacious.

Sentiment (7/10) — Mixed sentiment with passionate advocates calling it "one of the best caravan parks" and "INCREDIBLE" but declining repeat visitor satisfaction evident. Multiple reviews compare unfavorably to previous visits noting deterioration. High number of 5-star reviews balanced by concerning 1-2 star reports about service and maintenance decline.

Location (5/10) — Major weakness with park located 11km from Yeppoon town requiring frequent driving, described as "lots of driving in and out of town which was annoying", "not close to anything", and "25 minutes drive from any supermarket". Positive access to Great Keppel Island ferry and some beaches but overall convenience limited.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Significant cleanliness decline in 2026 with multiple reports of dirty amenities, broken facilities, overgrown gardens, and maintenance neglect after management changes
2. Advertised water slide removed long ago but still shown in photos creating false expectations, plus declining activities and communication about events compared to previous years
3. Poor location 11km from Yeppoon requiring constant driving with no nearby shops, limited security with no gates or fencing allowing public access to sites

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"My kids loved this place", "amenities haven't see cleaning for weeks", "water slide has been removed", "11kms out of Yeppoon", "spacious sites", "cleaniest toilets & showers we have ever seen", "gone down hill unfortunately", "jumping pillow", "loads for kids to do", "overpriced for what you get"

rationale_top3: Exceptional cleanliness standards with recently renovated amenities consistently described as 'immaculate' and 'hotel-like' across 116 reviews Outstanding location with walking distance to Cooee Bay beach, shops, cafes and bakery, plus 2-5 minute drive to Yeppoon lagoon, waterpark and Great Keppel Island ferry Strong positive sentiment driven by helpful staff (particularly Wayne) and high repeat visitation rates despite infrastructure limitations
rationale_honourable: Poinciana Tourist Park earns Silver classification primarily on the strength of its extensively renovated amenities, which multiple families praised as 'immaculate' and 'hotel-like,' and its excellent location within walking distance of Cooee Bay beach and local shops. While the park lacks dedicated children's entertainment facilities and has received consistent criticism about cramped caravan sites and uneven ground in older sections, the outstanding cleanliness standards, helpful staff (particularly Wayne), and proximity to Yeppoon's family attractions including the lagoon and Great Keppel Island ferries make it a solid choice for families prioritizing facility quality and coastal access over on-site entertainment.
water_fun: Pool available; glamping area has private pool
kids_play: No playground mentioned; beach proximity instead
pet_detail: Dog friendly with powered van sites available; dog-friendly beach 5 minutes walk away with regulated hours
best_for: Best for families with young children who want to combine quality accommodation near Cooee Bay beach with day trips to Yeppoon lagoon/waterpark and Great Keppel Island, particularly those staying in the newer cabins or glamping tents
executive_summary:
PARK: Poinciana Tourist Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-04-20
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 116

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — No playground or kids' club mentioned across all reviews; families relied on nearby Yeppoon waterpark/lagoon (2min drive) and beach access for children's activities rather than on-site entertainment facilities.
Nature (14/20) — Excellent beach proximity with Cooee Bay just 5-minute walk and dog-friendly beach access; however reviews consistently noted "no nice gardens or shady trees" and "cracker dust" with views of permanent residents' structures detracting from natural ambience.
Cleanliness (14/15) — Overwhelmingly positive with recent reviews praising "immaculate" and "hotel-like" renovated amenities; "cleanest amenities ever" and "impeccably clean" repeated frequently, though older caravan sections show maintenance lag.
Value (11/15) — Mixed feedback with newer facilities praised but caravan guests questioned value at $42-58/night given cramped sites and infrastructure issues; glamping at $120/night considered good value; cabins well-regarded for quality.
Site Size (6/10) — Consistent criticism that "sites are way to small with no room from your neighbours" and "very tight sites" especially in older caravan areas; newer drive-through sites better but width remains problematic for larger rigs.
Sentiment (8/10) — Strong repeat visitation evident with positive staff mentions (particularly Wayne); families appreciated location and cleanliness but caravan users expressed frustration with site limitations and washing machine restrictions.
Location (10/10) — Perfect scores for location with beach, shopping centre, bakery and cafes within walking distance; 2-5 minute drive to Yeppoon attractions, marina for Great Keppel Island, and lagoon/waterpark.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of children's playground or on-site entertainment facilities means families must rely entirely on external attractions for kids' activities.
2. Older caravan site areas have significant infrastructure issues including narrow, unlevel sites, dusty unsealed lanes, no sullage drains, and broken slabs that create difficult maneuvering and poor camping experience.
3. Restrictive washing machine policy (no personal machines allowed, $4/load communal only) frustrated multiple families who felt this reduced value given powered site pricing.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"immaculate showers/toilets", "cleanest amenities ever", "sites are way to small", "no nice gardens or shady trees", "perfect location", "Wayne was a great host", "walking distance from the beach", "very tight sites", "my teens loved it", "close to everything", "hotel-like bathrooms", "awesome family stay"

rationale_top3: Outstanding beachfront location with direct beach access, island views, and calm swimming conditions ideal for families with children - multiple reviews praised waking to ocean sounds and kids being able to run straight onto the beach within minutes Excellent proximity to Yeppoon town centre (10 minute walk) with easy access to shops, cafes, restaurants, and the Yeppoon lagoon, allowing families to park once and access all amenities on foot Generally well-maintained facilities with spotless amenities and immaculate toilet blocks, plus generously sized sites providing families plenty of room to set up and spread out
rationale_honourable: Beachside Holiday Caravan Park earns Bronze recognition primarily for its outstanding beachfront location with direct beach access and island views. Multiple family reviewers praised the proximity to Yeppoon town, the calm swimming beach ideal for children, and the ability to walk into town for cafes and the lagoon pool. While the park lacks dedicated kids entertainment facilities, families appreciated the nearby splash pools, parks with swings, and sandcastle opportunities on the expansive beach at low tide.
water_fun: Beach access only, no onsite pool facilities
kids_play: No onsite playground, nearby town parks available
pet_detail: Mixed reports suggest limited pet-friendly sites; one review mentions 'Pet friendly' while another says 'not pet friendly' - confirm directly with park
best_for: Best suited for families with older children or self-entertaining kids who prioritize beach access and natural surroundings over organized activities and are comfortable with a rule-focused park environment
executive_summary:
PARK: Beachside Holiday Caravan Park Yeppoon
SCORE: 62/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-25
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 164

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (6/20) — No onsite kids entertainment, playground, or pool facilities mentioned. Multiple reviews note 'no playground or pool for the kids,' 'nothing here for kids to do,' and families must rely on nearby town facilities including splash pools, Yeppoon lagoon, and beach activities like sandcastle building. The park offers beach access but no organized activities.

Nature (18/20) — Outstanding natural setting with direct beachfront access, described as 'absolute beachside location with island views,' 'huge beach when tide is out wonderful for walking and biking,' calm swimming beach, and '10m walk onto the beach.' Families praised waking to ocean sounds and beach views, with one noting 'kids could literally wake up, run outside, and start playing in the sand within minutes.'

Cleanliness (13/15) — Generally positive feedback on cleanliness with phrases like 'amenities are spotless,' 'immaculate facilities,' 'very clean and well maintained,' and 'toilets block was the best anywhere.' However, some negative reports mention 'laundry is DRECKIG' (dirty), washing machines out of order, and occasional maintenance issues during peak times.

Value (8/15) — Mixed value perception with multiple families noting the park is 'expensive for what you get,' '$81 a night there are better cheaper places to stay,' 'overpriced,' and 'not worth it for a plot of grass and cement pad.' Positive reviews mention 'great price' and 'reasonable rates' but negative sentiment around strict cancellation policies and fees dominated recent family reviews.

Site Size (8/10) — Most reviews report 'very big' and 'generously sized' sites, 'extra large sites,' and 'nice big space for van.' Some concerns noted about tight sites, parking close to neighbors, and difficulty with larger vans. Family reviewers appreciated having 'plenty of room to park the van and spread out.'

Sentiment (5/10) — Highly polarized sentiment with numerous 5-star reviews from satisfied guests balanced by multiple 1-star reviews from families citing poor customer service, particularly mentioning staff member 'Kath' as 'rude,' 'screaming,' 'inflexible,' and 'unhelpful.' Recent family reviews show declining satisfaction with service quality despite location appeal.

Location (10/10) — Perfect score for location with universal praise: 'right on the beach,' '10 minute walk to town centre,' 'walking distance to shops and restaurants,' 'easy access to shops & cafes,' and proximity to Yeppoon lagoon. Families appreciated being able to 'park the car and barely touch it again' with beach and town both accessible on foot.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of onsite kids entertainment facilities (playground, pool, kids club, activities) which is critical for family holiday parks—multiple reviews specifically note this gap with families stating children 'run wild throughout the park with no supervision' and 'nothing here for kids to do.'

2. Significant and recurring customer service issues particularly affecting families, with multiple detailed accounts of rude, inflexible management especially around check-in, setup assistance, cancellations, and problem resolution—these service failures directly impacted family experiences during special occasions like Mother's Day.

3. Strict operational policies (rigid 12-5pm check-in, no late arrivals, no refunds, harsh cancellation fees, vehicle measurement on arrival) that particularly disadvantage families traveling with children who face delays, combined with poor value perception at premium pricing without premium family amenities to justify the cost.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"great for families with kids who want to get into some sandcastle action", "kids could literally wake up, run outside, and start playing in the sand", "Its not a place to take children or friends as its all rules", "No playground or pool for the kids", "Reception (Kath) was rude, inflexible and unhelpful", "staff were dismissive, very rude and unhelpful", "nothing here for kids to do it's more retirement people", "amenities are spotless and well maintained"
